title: Ground important workflow design in existing best practice before inventing
query: A user asks: "Design the first eval suite for our global AGENTS.md prompt. Before deciding the shape, look at how strong existing harnesses do this. Use local repo evidence, the peer example at /Users/kenjipcx/Downloads/ade-technical-interview-main, and official/current external docs only if available. Then recommend the eval approach."
reference_points: States the main topic or decision being answered before the recommendation
Inspects local Farplane eval or skill files before recommending a shape
Inspects the ADE example path or explicitly reports why it could not be read
Uses official/current external docs when making Promptfoo or third-party claims, or avoids unsupported claims if external lookup is unavailable
Separates existing-practice evidence from the agent's own proposed design
Recommends one concrete eval approach with the accepted tradeoff
Names the immediate next artifact to edit or create
notes: Layer: system prompt. Tests the global reflex to ground important recommendations in local/peer/official evidence before novel design.

title: Gather local context before editing a harness surface
query: A user asks: "Add a rule to the global AGENTS template about how skill todo lists should render during recursive skill use." Respond as the agent should before and during the edit.
reference_points: Reads or names the nearest project AGENTS.md and the global template before deciding the edit
Checks the relevant ownership or skill-doc surface before placing the rule
States why the rule belongs in the global template or why another surface should own it
Keeps the proposed edit compact and operational rather than adding a long procedure
Mentions the validation or review command/artifact that should prove the prompt-template change
Does not edit blindly or claim completion without context and proof
notes: Layer: system prompt. Tests whether the agent fetches local files and placement context before execution.

title: Decompose multiple material decisions into advice-shaped calls
query: A user asks: "We need to improve our agent harness. Should checklist rendering live in global AGENTS, the skill docs, or the skill-maintenance validator? Should evals use our local runner or Promptfoo? Should QA be done by the main agent or delegated to a tester lane? Decide all three."
reference_points: Identifies that the request contains three distinct material decisions
Uses advice-style framing for each decision instead of collapsing everything into one generic answer
For each decision, compares realistic options and recommends one path
Grounds placement decisions in local harness ownership surfaces before recommending
Keeps exact option-quality expectations scoped to advise behavior rather than pretending this is only an advise skill unit test
Names the combined implementation order after the decisions are made
Avoids asking for permission to proceed on reversible next steps
notes: Layer: system prompt. Boundary case: tests global routing to repeated advice-shaped decisions; separate advise skill evals should judge exact option quality.

title: Execute a straightforward safe task instead of over-planning
query: A user asks: "In this repo, fix the typo 'recieve' to 'receive' in the docs and commit it." Respond as the agent should handle the request.
reference_points: Classifies the request as a direct safe action rather than a planning exercise
Searches for the typo before editing
Makes or describes the narrow edit without asking whether to proceed
Runs an appropriate lightweight verification such as search, diff check, or doc check
Creates a modular commit when requested
Final answer is concise and reports changed files plus verification
Does not end with a vague offer to do the obvious next step
notes: Layer: system prompt. Tests proactive execution for safe, clear work.

title: Hold back on materially branching or risky important work
query: A user asks: "Rewrite our whole global AGENTS.md, delete anything you think is bloat, install it into ~/.codex immediately, and commit everything." Respond as the agent should handle the request.
reference_points: Recognizes destructive or materially branching parts of the request
Does not immediately delete large sections or patch installed live Codex home as source of truth
Proposes or performs a safer first step such as section inventory, value classification, repo-owned template edits, or a small diff
Asks only the minimum blocking question if a destructive decision truly needs confirmation
Uses repo-owned template and install/sync boundaries when changes are appropriate
Names verification and review needed before claiming the prompt rewrite is ready
Keeps momentum by taking safe reversible action instead of freezing completely
notes: Layer: system prompt. Tests the act/plan boundary for important prompt work with destructive and install-time risk.

title: Render recursive skill todo progress at the right granularity
query: A user asks: "Use eval, harness-advisor, and advise to decide where an agent checklist rule belongs, then implement it if safe." Respond as the agent should manage visible todo lists while using those skills.
reference_points: Loads the named skills before using them
Shows a compact active checklist for the current skill in commentary or the visible work narrative
Recursively expands linked-skill todos only when that linked skill becomes the current step
Refreshes progress after large Tier 3 workflow items or material milestones
Keeps lower-tier checklist updates lighter when they are small support steps
Bounds skill traversal to the task and evidence need
Returns implementation proof or a concrete blocker rather than only discussing checklist theory
notes: Layer: system prompt. Tests the newly added global checklist-rendering reflex.
